title: The Tenant Who Never Existed
hook: Do not buy a house until you check behind the mirror. I just found out I’m not the only one living here.
setup: I pulled back the master bedroom glass. Behind it sat a hidden cabinet packed with decades of Polaroids.
escalation: Each photo shows the same man in my living room. The wallpaper changes, the furniture shifts, but he never ages.
climax: The final photo shows him holding a modern iPhone. The film is yellowed, yet the screen light is blindingly bright.
ending: I checked the blueprints, but this room was never built.
colorMood: faded sepia and cold digital white
visualMoments: POV of hands pulling a heavy mirror off a wall frame
Close up of dusty Polaroid stack being fanned out
Quick cuts of shifting living room decor through decades
Macro shot of a man holding a glowing modern phone in a grainy 70s photo
Static shot of the empty hidden room with the blueprints on the floor
